content.problemStatement:
Tyro and Dom are fighting over who makes the first problem of the contest. Both of them are very lazy so they don't want to make it.

Tyro has a patience level of AAA, meaning he will only be convinced on the AthA^{th}Ath time when Dom asks him to make the problem.

Dom, on the other hand, has decided to convince Tyro **at most** BBB times. If Tyro is not convinced by the BthB^{th}Bth time, Dom will proceed to make it himself.

Given the values of AAA and BBB, find out who will end up making the problem.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains 222 integers, AAA and BBB - Tyro's patience level, and the number of times Dom tries to convince Tyro respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `Dom` or `Tyro` corresponding to who ends up making the problem.


You can print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `DOM`, `dom`, `Dom`, and `dOM` are considered identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Dom will convince Tyro for at most 555 times. Tyro agrees on the 5th5^{th}5th time and makes the problem.

Explanation:
Dom will convince Tyro for at most 333 times. However, Tyro has decided to agree on the 4th4^{th}4th time. Thus, Dom makes the problem.
